content:
# Joshua Chapter 19 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio pages 242-244
**Source Image:** `JOS_019_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_019_page_002.jpg`, `JOS_019_page_003.jpg`

---

**19:1** And the second lot came out for Simeon, for the tribe of the Sons of Simeon according to their clans. And their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the Sons of Judah.

**19:2** And they had as their inheritance Beer-sheba, and Sheba, and Moladah,

**19:3** and Hazar-shual, and Balah, and Ezem,

**19:4** and Eltolad, and Bethul, and Hormah,

**19:5** and Ziklag, and Beth-marcaboth, and Hazar-susah,

**19:6** and Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen — thirteen cities and their villages.

**19:7** Ain, Rimmon, and Ether, and Ashan — four cities and their villages.

**19:8** And all the villages that were around these cities as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the Negev. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Simeon according to their clans.

**19:9** From the allotment of the Sons of Judah was the inheritance of the Sons of Simeon, for the portion of the Sons of Judah was too great for them. And the Sons of Simeon received their inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.

**19:10** And the third lot came up for the Sons of Zebulun according to their clans. And the border of their inheritance was as far as Sarid.

**19:11** And their border went up westward to Maralah, and reached to Dabbesheth, and reached to the wadi that is before Jokneam.

**19:12** And it turned from Sarid eastward, toward the rising of the sun, to the border of Chisloth-tabor, and went out to Daberath, and went up to Japhia.

**19:13** And from there it passed eastward to the east, to Gath-hepher, to Eth-kazin, and went out to Rimmon, which bends toward Neah.

**19:14** And the border went around it on the north to Hannathon, and its goings out were at the Valley of Iphtah-el.

**19:15** And Kattath, and Nahalal, and Shimron, and Idalah, and Bethlehem — twelve cities and their villages.

**19:16** This was the inheritance of the Sons of Zebulun according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

**19:17** For Issachar the fourth lot came out, for the Sons of Issachar according to their clans.

**19:18** And their territory was Jezreel, and Chesulloth, and Shunem,

**19:19** and Hapharaim, and Shion, and Anaharath,

**19:20** and Rabbith, and Kishion, and Ebez,

**19:21** and Remeth, and En-gannim, and En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez.

**19:22** And the border reached to Tabor, and Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and the goings out of their border were at the Jordan — sixteen cities and their villages.

**19:23** This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Issachar according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

**19:24** And the fifth lot came out for the tribe of the Sons of Asher according to their clans.

**19:25** And their territory was Helkath, and Hali, and Beten, and Achshaph,

**19:26** and Allammelech, and Amad, and Mishal. And it reached to Carmel on the west, and to Shihor-libnath.

**19:27** And it turned toward the rising of the sun to Beth-dagon, and reached to Zebulun and to the Valley of Iphtah-el northward, to Beth-emek and Neiel, and went out to Cabul on the left.

**19:28** And Ebron, and Rehob, and Hammon, and Kanah, as far as Great Sidon.

**19:29** And the border turned to Ramah, and as far as the fortified city of Tyre. And the border turned to Hosah, and its goings out were at the sea, from the region of Achzib.

**19:30** And Ummah, and Aphek, and Rehob — twenty-two cities and their villages.

**19:31** This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Asher according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

**19:32** For the Sons of Naphtali the sixth lot came out, for the Sons of Naphtali according to their clans.

**19:33** And their border was from Heleph, from the oak in Zaanannim, and Adami-nekeb, and Jabneel, as far as Lakkum, and its goings out were at the Jordan.

**19:34** And the border turned westward to Aznoth-tabor, and went out from there to Hukok, and reached to Zebulun on the south, and to Asher it reached on the west, and to Judah at the Jordan toward the rising of the sun.

**19:35** And the fortified cities were Ziddim, Zer, and Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth,

**19:36** and Adamah, and Ramah, and Hazor,

**19:37** and Kedesh, and Edrei, and En-hazor,

**19:38** and Iron, and Migdal-el, Horem, and Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh — nineteen cities and their villages.

**19:39** This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Naphtali according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

**19:40** For the tribe of the Sons of Dan according to their clans, the seventh lot came out.

**19:41** And the territory of their inheritance was Zorah, and Eshtaol, and Ir-shemesh,

**19:42** and Shaalabbin, and Aijalon, and Ithlah,

**19:43** and Elon, and Timnah, and Ekron,

**19:44** and Eltekeh, and Gibbethon, and Baalath,

**19:45** and Jehud, and Bene-berak, and Gath-rimmon,

**19:46** and Me-jarkon, and Rakkon, with the border opposite Joppa.

**19:47** And the territory of the Sons of Dan went out from them, and the Sons of Dan went up and fought against Leshem, and captured it, and struck it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it, and dwelt in it. And they called Leshem "Dan," after the name of Dan their father.

**19:48** This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Dan according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

**19:49** And they finished dividing the land as an inheritance by its borders. And the Sons of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua son of Nun in their midst.

**19:50** By the command of YHWH they gave him the city that he asked for — Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim. And he built the city and dwelt in it.

**19:51** These are the inheritances that Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the Sons of Israel distributed by lot in Shiloh before YHWH, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. And they finished dividing the land.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

### Method
Each verse was compared across all three translations (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) and checked against the manuscript images of Leningrad Codex pages 242-244. Where all three translators agreed on a reading, that agreement was adopted. Where they diverged, the manuscript was consulted and the wisest synthesis was chosen, favoring accuracy to the Hebrew while maintaining readable English. No Codex Vaticanus was available for this chapter.

### Agreements
The three translators demonstrated exceptionally high agreement across Joshua 19. This chapter consists primarily of boundary descriptions and city lists, which are relatively formulaic and leave little room for divergence. All three preserve:
- **YHWH** (not "the LORD") in vv. 50-51
- **Sons of Israel / Sons of [tribe name]** (not "Israelites" / "[tribe]-ites") throughout
- **Tent of Meeting** (not "tabernacle") in v. 51
- The **city counts** (thirteen, four, twelve, sixteen, twenty-two, nineteen) as stated in the MT
- **Beer-sheba and Sheba** as two separate entries in v. 2, following the MT against the LXX
- **Ain and Rimmon** as two separate cities in v. 7, following the MT (cf. 1 Chr. 4:32 "En-rimmon")
- **Leshem** (not "Laish" as in Judges 18) in v. 47, following the MT form
- **Timnath-serah** (not "Timnath-heres" as in Judges 2:9) in v. 50, following the Leningrad Codex
- The **inclusio** with Joshua 18:1, framing the entire allotment ceremony at Shiloh

### Major Decisions

**1. Proper name rendering: anglicized forms (throughout)**
EMET uses Hebrew transliterations throughout (Shimon, Yehudah, Zevulun, Yissakhar, Yarden, Yehoshua, etc.) while LOGOS and PNEUMA use standard anglicized forms (Simeon, Judah, Zebulun, Issachar, Jordan, Joshua, etc.). SOPHIA adopts anglicized forms, consistent with the established convention from prior chapters: these proper nouns have universally established English equivalents. The divine name YHWH is preserved because no adequate English equivalent exists. See Disagreement Log.

**2. "The second lot came out" vs. "The second lot fell to" (v. 1)**
EMET and LOGOS render "came out" (from Hebrew yatsa'), while PNEUMA renders "fell to." The Hebrew verb is yatsa' ("went out / came out"), describing the lot emerging from the vessel. SOPHIA adopts "came out" as the literal and correct rendering. See Disagreement Log.

**3. "And" conjunction chains vs. compression (throughout)**
EMET preserves every waw conjunction ("And they had... and Chatzar-Shual and Balah..."). LOGOS preserves most conjunctions with lighter punctuation. PNEUMA compresses formulaic lists by dropping leading conjunctions and using commas. SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew conjunction style with "and" throughout the city lists, per project rules requiring Hebrew conjunction style. See Disagreement Log.

**4. "Their border was" vs. "their territory included" (vv. 18, 25, 41)**
EMET renders "their border was" in most cases. LOGOS and PNEUMA use "their territory included" for verses describing city lists rather than boundary lines. The Hebrew varies: v. 18 uses gevulam ("their border/territory"), while the context describes included cities rather than border points. SOPHIA uses "their territory was" — preserving the Hebrew noun gevul while acknowledging that the syntax describes territorial holdings. See Disagreement Log.

**5. "Goings out" vs. "outgoings" vs. "ending at" (vv. 14, 22, 29, 33)**
EMET renders totse'ot as "goings out," LOGOS as "outgoings," and PNEUMA as "ending at" or "coming out at." SOPHIA adopts "goings out" — the most literal rendering of the Hebrew totse'ot (from yatsa'), preserving the technical geographical term used throughout Joshua's boundary descriptions. See Disagreement Log.

**6. "On the left" vs. "on the north" (v. 27)**
EMET preserves the Hebrew idiom "on the left" (mi-semo'l), which in Hebrew orientation (facing east) means "north." LOGOS preserves "on the left." PNEUMA renders the meaning as "on the north." SOPHIA preserves "on the left" — the Hebrew directional idiom is comprehensible with a note, and preserving it maintains the embodied spatial language of the Hebrew text. See Disagreement Log.

**7. "Went out from them" vs. "went out beyond them" vs. "was too small for them" (v. 47)**
EMET renders "the border of the sons of Dan went out from them" (literal). LOGOS renders "went out beyond them." PNEUMA paraphrases as "the territory of Dan was too small for them." The Hebrew va-yetse' gevul-benei-Dan mehem literally says "went out from them" — the territory slipped from their grasp. SOPHIA adopts "went out from them" (EMET), which is the literal Hebrew and allows the reader to encounter the same ambiguity as the original audience. See Disagreement Log.

**8. "By the word of YHWH" vs. "By the command of YHWH" (v. 50)**
EMET renders al-pi YHWH as "by the word of YHWH." LOGOS renders "by the command of YHWH." PNEUMA renders "by YHWH's command." The Hebrew al-pi literally means "upon the mouth of," which can mean "word," "command," or "instruction." SOPHIA adopts "by the command of YHWH" (LOGOS) — the phrase in context refers to an authoritative directive, and "command" captures that sense while "word" is too generic. See Disagreement Log.

**9. "Dividing" vs. "distributing" vs. "dividing" (vv. 49, 51)**
EMET uses "dividing" (v. 49) and "dividing" (v. 51). LOGOS uses "distributing" (v. 49) and "distributed" (v. 51). PNEUMA uses "distributing" (v. 49) and "completed the division" (v. 51). The Hebrew root is n-ch-l (nachal), relating to inheritance/possession. SOPHIA uses "dividing" for consistency and because the Hebrew concept is division of land into inheritance portions. See Disagreement Log.

**10. "So" vs. "And" connectors (vv. 9, 51)**
LOGOS uses "So" to introduce the result clauses in v. 9 ("So the Sons of Simeon received...") and v. 51 ("So they finished..."). EMET uses "and" throughout. PNEUMA varies. SOPHIA uses "And" per Hebrew conjunction style rules — the Hebrew waw does not distinguish causal from sequential connection, and the SOPHIA convention preserves this. See Disagreement Log.

**11. "Ha-Tziddim" vs. "Ziddim" (v. 35)**
EMET preserves the definite article: "ha-Tziddim." LOGOS and PNEUMA render simply "Ziddim." The Leningrad Codex reads הַצִּדִּים with the definite article. SOPHIA adopts "Ziddim" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) — in English, the definite article is typically absorbed into proper nouns in biblical place names, following the convention used throughout Joshua (e.g., "the Negev" is kept, but city names drop the article). See Disagreement Log.

**12. "Built" vs. "rebuilt" (v. 50)**
EMET and LOGOS render "he built the city" (va-yiven). PNEUMA renders "he rebuilt the city." The Hebrew is simply banah ("built") with no prefix indicating "re-." SOPHIA adopts "built" (EMET/LOGOS) as the literal reading. See Disagreement Log.

### Manuscript Observations
The Leningrad Codex pages 242-244 are legible and well-preserved for Joshua 19. The three-column layout is clearly visible with Masoretic annotations in the margins. No significant textual variants were observed between the manuscript images and the translations provided. The chapter's primarily geographic and list-based content results in minimal interpretive difficulty at the textual level. The parashah markers are visible, confirming the traditional division points.

---

## Disagreement Log

### 1. Proper Name Transliteration vs. Anglicization (throughout)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Shimon, Yehudah, Zevulun, Yissakhar, Yarden, Yehoshua" | Hebrew transliteration |
| LOGOS | "Simeon, Judah, Zebulun, Issachar, Jordan, Joshua" | Standard anglicized |
| PNEUMA | "Simeon, Judah, Zebulun, Issachar, Jordan, Joshua" | Standard anglicized |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Simeon, Judah, Zebulun, Issachar, Jordan, Joshua" — LOGOS/PNEUMA upheld (2-1)** | These proper nouns have universally established English equivalents. YHWH is preserved per project convention because no adequate English equivalent exists, but tribal and geographic names with long-standing English forms are anglicized for readability. |

### 2. "Came out" vs. "fell to" (vv. 1, 17, 24, 32, 40)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the second lot came out for Shimon" | yatsa' = came out |
| LOGOS | "the second lot came out for Simeon" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "The second lot fell to Simeon" | Dynamic: lot "fell to" the recipient |
| **SOPHIA** | **"the second lot came out for Simeon" — EMET/LOGOS upheld (2-1)** | The Hebrew verb is yatsa' ("went out / came out"), not naphal ("fell"). The lot was drawn from a vessel; "came out" preserves the physical action described. |

### 3. Conjunction Style: Preserved vs. Compressed (throughout)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "and Chatzar-Shual and Balah and Etzem" | Every waw preserved |
| LOGOS | "and Hazar-shual, and Balah, and Ezem" | Conjunctions with commas |
| PNEUMA | "Hazar-shual, Balah, Ezem" | Commas only, conjunctions dropped |
| **SOPHIA** | **"and Hazar-shual, and Balah, and Ezem" — LOGOS upheld** | Hebrew conjunction style is required per project rules. LOGOS's approach preserves each "and" while adding commas for English readability. PNEUMA's compression loses the Hebrew waw chains. |

### 4. "Their border was" vs. "their territory included" (vv. 18, 25, 41)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "their border was [to] Yizreel" | gevulam = their border |
| LOGOS | "their territory included Jezreel" | gevulam = territory (contextual) |
| PNEUMA | "Their territory included Jezreel" | Same as LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"their territory was Jezreel" — Blend** | "Territory" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) better captures the sense of gevul in context, as these verses list cities within the allotment rather than tracing a boundary line. But "was" (rather than "included") preserves the Hebrew verbless clause more closely than "included." |

### 5. "Goings out" vs. "outgoings" vs. "ending at" (vv. 14, 22, 29, 33)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "its goings out were the valley of Yiphtach-El" | totse'otav = its goings out |
| LOGOS | "its outgoings were at the Valley of Iphtah-el" | totse'otav = outgoings |
| PNEUMA | "ending at the Valley of Iphtah-el" | Dynamic paraphrase |
| **SOPHIA** | **"its goings out were at the Valley of Iphtah-el" — Blend (EMET phrasing + LOGOS preposition)** | "Goings out" preserves the Hebrew totse'ot more transparently than "outgoings." The preposition "at" (LOGOS) clarifies the geographical sense. |

### 6. "On the left" vs. "on the north" (v. 27)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "went out to Kavul on the left" | mi-semo'l = on the left |
| LOGOS | "went out to Cabul on the left" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "continuing out to Cabul on the north" | Renders the meaning |
| **SOPHIA** | **"went out to Cabul on the left" — EMET/LOGOS upheld (2-1)** | The Hebrew idiom "on the left" (facing east = north) is preserved. The SOPHIA Bible preserves embodied directional language where comprehensible. |

### 7. "Went out from them" vs. "went out beyond them" vs. "was too small" (v. 47)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the border of the sons of Dan went out from them" | Literal: va-yetse'... mehem |
| LOGOS | "the territory of the Sons of Dan went out beyond them" | "beyond" interprets mehem |
| PNEUMA | "the territory of Dan was too small for them" | Full paraphrase of the idiom |
| **SOPHIA** | **"the territory of the Sons of Dan went out from them" — Blend (LOGOS noun + EMET verb phrase)** | "Territory" (LOGOS) better renders gevul in this context than "border" (EMET). "Went out from them" preserves the literal Hebrew without the interpretive addition of "beyond" (LOGOS) or the full paraphrase (PNEUMA). The ambiguity of the Hebrew is preserved. |

### 8. "By the word of YHWH" vs. "by the command of YHWH" (v. 50)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "By the word of YHWH" | al-pi = upon the mouth of → word |
| LOGOS | "By the command of YHWH" | al-pi = command |
| PNEUMA | "By YHWH's command" | Same sense, different word order |
| **SOPHIA** | **"By the command of YHWH" — LOGOS upheld** | al-pi YHWH in context refers to an authoritative directive, not mere speech. "Command" captures the force of the idiom. PNEUMA's word order ("YHWH's command") departs from Hebrew syntax. |

### 9. "Dividing" vs. "distributing" (vv. 49, 51)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "they finished dividing the land" | chilqu = dividing |
| LOGOS | "they finished distributing the land" | chilqu = distributing |
| PNEUMA | "they completed the division of the land" | Dynamic restructuring |
| **SOPHIA** | **"they finished dividing the land" — EMET upheld** | The Hebrew root ch-l-q means "to divide, apportion." "Dividing" is the more precise rendering and maintains consistency with the land-division theme of Joshua 13-21. |

### 10. "So" vs. "And" Connectors (vv. 9, 51)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "and the sons of Shimon received" | waw = and |
| LOGOS | "So the Sons of Simeon received" | waw = So (causal) |
| PNEUMA | "So the sons of Simeon received" | Same as LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"And the Sons of Simeon received" — EMET upheld** | The Hebrew waw-consecutive does not encode causality. "And" preserves the undetermined connector per Hebrew conjunction style rules. |

### 11. "Ha-Tziddim" vs. "Ziddim" (v. 35)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "ha-Tziddim" | Preserves definite article |
| LOGOS | "Ziddim" | Drops article per English convention |
| PNEUMA | "Ziddim" | Same as LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Ziddim" — LOGOS/PNEUMA upheld (2-1)** | While the MT reads הַצִּדִּים with the article, English convention absorbs the article into the proper noun. This follows the pattern used for other place names (e.g., ha-Ai becomes "Ai" in standard rendering). |

### 12. "Built" vs. "rebuilt" (v. 50)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "he built the city" | va-yiven = and he built |
| LOGOS | "he built the city" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "He rebuilt the city" | Adds "re-" prefix (interpretive) |
| **SOPHIA** | **"he built the city" — EMET/LOGOS upheld (2-1)** | The Hebrew is simply banah ("built"). PNEUMA's "rebuilt" is an interpretation (assuming the city pre-existed and was restored), but the text does not contain the re- prefix. |

### 13. "Even to Maralah" vs. "to Maralah" (v. 11)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "and [to] Maralah" | Adds directional marker |
| LOGOS | "even to Maralah" | Adds intensifier "even" |
| PNEUMA | "to Maralah" | Simple preposition |
| **SOPHIA** | **"to Maralah" — PNEUMA upheld** | The Hebrew has a simple directional marker. "Even" (LOGOS) adds rhetorical emphasis not present in the Hebrew. |

### 14. "As far as" vs. "to" (v. 29)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "and as far as the fortified city of Tzor" | ad = as far as |
| LOGOS | "and to the fortified city of Tyre" | ad = to |
| PNEUMA | "and on to the fortified city of Tyre" | ad = on to |
| **SOPHIA** | **"and as far as the fortified city of Tyre" — EMET upheld** | The Hebrew ad conveys extent ("as far as"), not mere direction ("to"). This nuance matters geographically — the border reached all the way to Tyre. |

### 15. "Struck it with the edge of the sword" — unanimous (v. 47)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "struck it with the edge of the sword" | le-fi cherev = to the mouth of the sword |
| LOGOS | "struck it with the edge of the sword" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "put it to the sword" | Compressed idiom |
| **SOPHIA** | **"struck it with the edge of the sword" — EMET/LOGOS upheld (2-1)** | The Hebrew idiom le-fi cherev ("to the mouth of the sword") is rendered as "edge of the sword" — a well-established English equivalent that preserves the concrete imagery. PNEUMA's "put it to the sword" loses the specificity. |
